title: Greens call for ban on US military using Prestwick Airport

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: Prestwick is often used by military aircraft for refuelling PA Media The US military should be banned from using Glasgow Prestwick Airport, the Scottish Greens have said ahead of a debate at Holyrood this week. The party will use its parliamentary time to call on the US military to be barred from publicly-owned airports, saying Holyrood must “stand up to Trump”.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: Military aircraft regularly use Prestwick, which offers refuelling services and rest facilities for crews. The South Ayrshire airport, which was nationalised in 2013, says it can cater for military aircraft of all sizes day and night. When US President Donald Trump visited Scotland last summer, Air Force One landed at Prestwick. Scottish Green co-leader Gillian Mackay said: “Donald Trump is no friend of Scotland. He has shown total contempt for our courts and for human rights around the world.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: Scottish Green co-leader Gillian Mackay urged the Government to act to ensure ‘Scotland’s infrastructure is not used to boost Trump’. PA Media

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – Greens call for ban on US military using Prestwick Airport
